g.data-narration: Welcome to Operating System Services - the fundamental functions that make computing possible.
An OS Service is a function the operating system provides to users and programs, making computers easy and efficient to use.
g.text: A function provided by the OS that performs specific tasks for
users or applications, making computing convenient and efficient.

g.data-narration: The OS sits between hardware and users, providing services that abstract complex hardware details.
Process Management handles creating, scheduling, and terminating processes - programs in execution.
g.text: PID | State | PC | Registers | Memory Info

g.data-narration: Memory Management allocates RAM to processes, implements virtual memory, and protects process memory spaces.
g.text: • Virtual Memory (extends RAM using disk space)

g.data-narration: File Management creates an abstraction over storage, organizing data in files and directories with protection.
Device Management handles all I/O devices through drivers, using buffering, caching, and spooling for efficiency.
g.text: • Spooling (queues print jobs - Simultaneous Peripheral Operations)

g.data-narration: Security Services protect system resources through authentication, access control, and encryption mechanisms.
g.text: • Access Control (permissions based on user identity)
• Encryption (protect data at rest and in transit)

g.data-narration: Networking enables communication between computers through protocols like TCP/IP and socket programming.
User Interface provides ways for users to interact with the system - CLI, GUI, or touch-based interfaces.
g.text: • CLI (Command Line Interface) - text-based, e.g., Bash, CMD
• GUI (Graphical User Interface) - windows, icons, menus, pointers

g.data-narration: Error Detection monitors the system for problems and takes recovery actions to maintain stability.
g.text: • Error Detection (checksums, parity bits, exception handling)
• Recovery Actions (terminate faulty processes, restart services)

g.data-narration: Secondary Storage Management handles disk space, implements file systems, and optimizes disk access through scheduling.
g.text: • Free Space Management (bit vectors, free lists)

g.data-narration: These ten services work together to make the computer system convenient, efficient, and secure for users.
g.text: All 10 OS Services at a Glance

g.data-narration: Remember these important formulas for exams - address translation in paging and disk scheduling calculations.
Final exam tips: Know all 10 services, understand system calls vs services, and remember process vs program difference.
g.text: ✓ System calls (fork, exec, open) access OS services
✓ Program = passive file, Process = active execution
✓ Kernel provides services via system calls

g.data-narration: OS Services are the foundation of computing - understanding them is essential for every computer science student.
g.text: 🎓 Understanding OS Services is Essential for Computer Science!
